docs.title: petstore-openapi-client
docs.text:
A production-ready Petstore API built with FastAPI.

This Python package is automatically generated by the OpenAPI Generator project:

  • API version: v1
  • Package version: 0.0.0
  • Generator version: 7.23.0-SNAPSHOT
  • Build package: org.openapitools.codegen.languages.PythonPydanticV1ClientCodegen

docs.title: Requirements.
docs.text: Python 3.7+

docs.title: Install with uv
docs.text:
Install runtime dependencies and the package in editable mode:

uv venv
uv pip install -r requirements.txt
uv pip install -e .

You can also install directly from Git:

uv pip install git+https://github.com/RamonAlcantaraArceo/petstore-openapi-client-pydantic.git

Then import the package: 

import petstore_openapi_client

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
Execute tests with:

uv pip install -r test-requirements.txt
uv run pytest

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Please follow the installation procedure and then run the following:

import asyncio 
import time
import petstore_openapi_client
from petstore_openapi_client.rest import ApiException
from pprint import pprint

# Defining the host is optional and defaults to http://localhost:8000
# See configuration.py for a list of all supported configuration parameters.
configuration = petstore_openapi_client.Configuration(
    host = "http://localhost:8000"
)


async def main():

    # Enter a context with an instance of the API client
    async with petstore_openapi_client.ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
        # Create an instance of the API class
        api_instance = petstore_openapi_client.HealthApi(api_client)

        try:
            # Health Check
            api_response = await api_instance.health_check_api_v1_health_get()
            print("The response of HealthApi->health_check_api_v1_health_get:\n")
            pprint(api_response)
        except ApiException as e:
            print("Exception when calling HealthApi->health_check_api_v1_health_get: %s\n" % e)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: health_check_api_v1_health_get
docs.text:
HealthResponse health_check_api_v1_health_get()

Health Check

Return service health status.

Returns: Typed health payload with runtime mode and build metadata.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
import asyncio 
import time
import os
import petstore_openapi_client
from petstore_openapi_client.models.health_response import HealthResponse
from petstore_openapi_client.rest import ApiException
from pprint import pprint

# Defining the host is optional and defaults to http://localhost:8000
# See configuration.py for a list of all supported configuration parameters.
configuration = petstore_openapi_client.Configuration(
    host = "http://localhost:8000"
)


async def main():

    # Enter a context with an instance of the API client
    async with petstore_openapi_client.ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
        # Create an instance of the API class
        api_instance = petstore_openapi_client.HealthApi(api_client)

        try:
            # Health Check
            api_response = await api_instance.health_check_api_v1_health_get()
            print("The response of HealthApi->health_check_api_v1_health_get:\n")
            pprint(api_response)
        except Exception as e:
            print("Exception when calling HealthApi->health_check_api_v1_health_get: %s\n" % e)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: add_pet
docs.text:
Pet add_pet(pet_create)

Add Pet

Add a new pet to the store.

Args: pet: Pet data from request body. service: Injected PetService.

Returns: The created pet.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
• Api Key Authentication (APIKeyHeader): 
    import asyncio 
import time
import os
import petstore_openapi_client
from petstore_openapi_client.models.pet import Pet
from petstore_openapi_client.models.pet_create import PetCreate
from petstore_openapi_client.rest import ApiException
from pprint import pprint

# Defining the host is optional and defaults to http://localhost:8000
# See configuration.py for a list of all supported configuration parameters.
configuration = petstore_openapi_client.Configuration(
    host = "http://localhost:8000"
)

# The client must configure the authentication and authorization parameters
# in accordance with the API server security policy.
# Examples for each auth method are provided below, use the example that
# satisfies your auth use case.

# Configure API key authorization: APIKeyHeader
configuration.api_key['APIKeyHeader'] = os.environ["API_KEY"]

# Uncomment below to setup prefix (e.g. Bearer) for API key, if needed
# configuration.api_key_prefix['APIKeyHeader'] = 'Bearer'

async def main():

    # Enter a context with an instance of the API client
    async with petstore_openapi_client.ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
        # Create an instance of the API class
        api_instance = petstore_openapi_client.PetApi(api_client)
        pet_create = petstore_openapi_client.PetCreate() # PetCreate | 

        try:
            # Add Pet
            api_response = await api_instance.add_pet(pet_create)
            print("The response of PetApi->add_pet:\n")
            pprint(api_response)
        except Exception as e:
            print("Exception when calling PetApi->add_pet: %s\n" % e)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(main())

Update Pet

Update an existing pet.

Args: pet: Updated pet data from request body. service: Injected PetService.

Returns: The updated pet.

docs.text:
• Api Key Authentication (APIKeyHeader): 
    import asyncio 
import time
import os
import petstore_openapi_client
from petstore_openapi_client.models.pet import Pet
from petstore_openapi_client.models.pet_update import PetUpdate
from petstore_openapi_client.rest import ApiException
from pprint import pprint

# Defining the host is optional and defaults to http://localhost:8000
# See configuration.py for a list of all supported configuration parameters.
configuration = petstore_openapi_client.Configuration(
    host = "http://localhost:8000"
)

# The client must configure the authentication and authorization parameters
# in accordance with the API server security policy.

configuration.api_key['APIKeyHeader'] = os.environ["API_KEY"]

# Uncomment below to setup prefix (e.g. Bearer) for API key, if needed
# configuration.api_key_prefix['APIKeyHeader'] = 'Bearer'

async def main():

    # Enter a context with an instance of the API client
    async with petstore_openapi_client.ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
        # Create an instance of the API class
        api_instance = petstore_openapi_client.PetApi(api_client)
        pet_update = petstore_openapi_client.PetUpdate() # PetUpdate | 

        try:
            # Update Pet
            api_response = await api_instance.update_pet(pet_update)
            print("The response of PetApi->update_pet:\n")
            pprint(api_response)
        except Exception as e:
            print("Exception when calling PetApi->update_pet: %s\n" % e)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(main())
